matrix_contract.allowed: Use only for contact-gate contrast across nominal-only pilot and five-profile benchmark evidence.
matrix_contract.not_allowed: Do not read this matrix as a mixed-contract leaderboard or direct across-row ranking.
matrix_contract.row_source_rule: Each row cites the direct confirm or benchmark JSON input passed to the exporter; do not infer row provenance from separate paper table exports.

rows.label: PPO w/o BC
rows.allowed_claim: Pure RL stays outside the useful-contact gate under the nominal-only pilot contract.
rows.not_allowed_claim: Do not claim this method reaches useful contact or compare it as a mixed-contract leaderboard winner.

rows.label: SAC w/o BC
rows.allowed_claim: Best pure-RL distance proxy under the nominal-only pilot contract, but still zero-contact.
rows.not_allowed_claim: Do not claim SAC w/o BC solves insertion, enters useful contact, or wins a mixed-contract leaderboard.

rows.label: TD3 w/o BC
rows.allowed_claim: Pure RL stays outside the useful-contact gate under the nominal-only pilot contract.
rows.not_allowed_claim: Do not claim this method reaches useful contact or compare it as a mixed-contract leaderboard winner.

rows.label: BC-only (stable 32/32)
rows.allowed_claim: Demonstration support alone reopens contact and near-ceiling success under the five-profile benchmark.
rows.not_allowed_claim: Do not compare this row as a mixed-contract leaderboard winner against nominal-only pure-RL pilot rows.

rows.label: BC -> PPO
rows.allowed_claim: Demo-supported RL reopens contact and non-zero success under the five-profile benchmark.
rows.not_allowed_claim: Do not compare this row as a mixed-contract leaderboard winner against nominal-only pure-RL pilot rows.

rows.label: DAPG-lite
rows.allowed_claim: Demo-supported RL reopens contact and non-zero success under the five-profile benchmark.
rows.not_allowed_claim: Do not compare this row as a mixed-contract leaderboard winner against nominal-only pure-RL pilot rows.

rows.label: Fixed-impedance RL (stable BC 32/32)
rows.allowed_claim: Fixed impedance still enters contact and succeeds under support, so this row acts as a mechanics anchor.
rows.not_allowed_claim: Do not read this row as proof that impedance type alone determines learnability or as a mixed-contract leaderboard winner.
